ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "emp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Home address of the employee */,
  "employee_type" TEXT /* Type of employee (e.g., full-time, part-time, contractor) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the employee (e.g., active, terminated) */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  "job_function_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job function of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to */,
  "division_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the division the department is part of */,
  "salary" REAL /* Salary of the employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started working */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was terminated (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("emp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_division_id FOREIGN KEY ("division_id") REFERENCES divisions ("division_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("emp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_function_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_function_id") REFERENCES job_functions ("job_function_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "division_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the division the department is part of */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department's manager */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the department */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated to the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_division_id FOREIGN KEY ("division_id") REFERENCES divisions ("division_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("emp_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "divisions" (
  "division_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each division */,
  "division_name" TEXT /* Name of the division */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the division's manager */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the division */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated to the division */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("division_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_divisions_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("emp_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_functions" (
  "job_function_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job function */,
  "job_function_name" TEXT /* Name of the job function */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job function */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department associated with the job function */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the job function */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_function_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_functions_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary entry */,
  "emp_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "salary" REAL /* Salary amount */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date the salary became effective */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the salary became ineffective */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the salary change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salaries_emp_id FOREIGN KEY ("emp_id") REFERENCES employees ("emp_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit entry */,
  "emp_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "benefit_type" TEXT /* Type of benefit (e.g., health insurance, retirement plan) */,
  "provider" TEXT /* Provider of the benefit */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the benefit became effective */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the benefit became ineffective */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benefits_emp_id FOREIGN KEY ("emp_id") REFERENCES employees ("emp_id")
)
column_names: Type of employee (e.g., full-time, part-time, contractor)
Current status of the employee (e.g., active, terminated)
Reference to the job function of the employee
Reference to the department the employee belongs to
Reference to the division the department is part of
Date the employee was terminated (if applicable)
Reference to the division the department is part of

Reference to the department associated with the job function
